title: aictrl Workflow (v1)
description: Declarative, YAML/JSON-authored workflow definition. Layer 1 (this schema) validates structure; layer 2 validates each node's `inputs` against the referenced template's parameter schema at load time. See reference/authoring-guide.md for the `when`/`until`/`while` expression language (CEL).

triggers.description: File-declared event triggers. Each trigger fires the workflow automatically when its event fires. Supports type:label, type:comment, type:pr-ready, type:pr-opened, and type:chat-message; up to 10 triggers per workflow.

template.description: Portable template ref: the template's org-unique kebab `name` (e.g. "inline-code-review"). Resolved org-scoped to a templateId at load; layer-2 MUST confirm an active template with this name exists — see reference/authoring-guide.md.

workflow.description: Portable composite-workflow ref: the workflow's org-unique kebab `name` (e.g. "release-pipeline"). Resolved org-scoped at load.

when.description: CEL boolean expr; node is skipped when false

maxIterations.description: loop: hard per-loop iteration cap (required for type=loop)

until.description: loop: CEL exit condition, evaluated after each pass (do-while). Mutually exclusive with `while`.

while.description: loop: CEL continue condition, evaluated before each pass. Mutually exclusive with `until`.

body.description: Loop body subgraph. Per-loop cap is `maxIterations` (<=25); max nesting depth is 3. WORST CASE at the depth cap is the product of nested maxIterations (e.g. 25^3 = 15,625 leaf executions). A NORMATIVE global iteration budget (product of all nested maxIterations on any path <= 1000) is enforced at load by DAG validation; runs exceeding it are rejected.

signalSource.description: wait: opaque signal identifier matched by pure in-memory lookup. Charset forbids ':' and '/', so it can never be a URL/host the platform fetches (no SSRF).

oneOf.description: Upstream node output; no extract = full artifact
shorthand equivalent to { method: json-path, expression: <string> }; use the object form for full

method.description: v1 supports full | json-path. regex/template are deferred until a linear-time matcher (RE2) and a non-evaluating template grammar land — they are ReDoS/injection surfaces on untrusted, PR-derived content.

condition.description: auto gates: CEL boolean expr (required when type=auto)

fileTrigger.description: A file-declared trigger in the workflow `triggers:` block. Supported `type` values: `label`, `comment`, `pr-ready`, `pr-opened`, and `chat-message`; any other type is rejected at load.

chatMessageTrigger.description: File-declared chat-message trigger: fires the workflow when a chat provider receives a message whose first token equals `command`. It is not PR-scoped and has no `on` field. Unlike label, comment, and PR triggers, `inputs` maps workflow parameter names to `{ from, field }` objects (a chat event source and a dot-path), not JSONPath strings.

provider.description: Chat provider. Telegram has runtime support; Slack and Discord definitions can be stored but do not yet fire workflows.

command.description: Slash command that fires this trigger when it is the first whitespace-delimited token of a chat message (e.g. `/run-review`). Same grammar as the comment trigger; matched exactly and case-sensitively.

chats.description: Optional allowlist of chat or channel ids this trigger fires for. Integers (Telegram) or strings (Slack and Discord) are stored as text. Omit to fire for any chat. Max 100 entries.

acceptPhotos.description: Whether photo attachments on the triggering message are accepted as inputs. Default true.

sendConfirmation.description: Whether to post an acknowledgement message back to the chat when the run starts. Default true.

sendCompletion.description: Whether to post a completion message back to the chat when the run finishes. Default false.

inputs.description: Maps workflow parameter names (keys) to chat input mappings (values). Each value is a `{ from, field }` object, not a JSONPath string. Max 20 entries.

additionalProperties.description: A chat input mapping: `from` selects the event source object, and `field` is a dot-path into it.

from.description: The chat event source object the `field` dot-path reads from.

field.description: Dot-path into the `from` object (e.g. `text` or `photo.0.file_id`), not a JSONPath expression.

commentTrigger.description: File-declared comment trigger: fires the workflow when an `issue_comment.created` event arrives on a PR and the comment's first token equals `command`. `inputs` maps workflow parameter names to JSONPath expressions evaluated against the GitHub `issue_comment` webhook payload. SECURITY (enforced at event time): only comments from a repo collaborator with write/admin permission fire the trigger; bot comments and comment edits are ignored.

on.description: The event category. Must be `pull-request`; the comment must be on a PR (issue comments on non-PR issues are ignored).

command.description: Slash command that fires this trigger when it is the first whitespace-delimited token of a PR comment (e.g. `/review-fix`). Matched exactly and case-sensitively — `/review-fix` fires but `/review-fixes` does not.

inputs.description: Maps workflow parameter names (keys) to JSONPath expressions (values) evaluated against the `issue_comment.created` webhook payload. Unresolvable expressions throw at fire time. Max 20 entries.

labelTrigger.description: File-declared label trigger: fires the workflow when `pull_request.labeled` arrives with a matching label. `inputs` maps workflow parameter names to JSONPath expressions evaluated against the GitHub webhook payload.

on.description: The event category. Must be `pull-request`; labels on other event types are not supported in slice 1.

label.description: Exact GitHub label name to match (case-sensitive).

inputs.description: Maps workflow parameter names (keys) to JSONPath expressions (values) that are evaluated against the `pull_request.labeled` webhook payload. Unresolvable expressions throw at fire time. Max 20 entries.

prReadyTrigger.description: File-declared pr-ready trigger: fires the workflow when a PR becomes ready for review (`pull_request.ready_for_review`) or is opened as a non-draft (`pull_request.opened` with draft:false). A draft open does not fire this trigger. `inputs` maps workflow parameter names to JSONPath expressions evaluated against the `pull_request` webhook payload.

inputs.description: Maps workflow parameter names (keys) to JSONPath expressions (values) evaluated against the `pull_request` webhook payload. Unresolvable expressions throw at fire time. Max 20 entries.

prOpenedTrigger.description: File-declared pr-opened trigger: fires the workflow when a PR is opened (`pull_request.opened`), including draft opens. `inputs` maps workflow parameter names to JSONPath expressions evaluated against the `pull_request` webhook payload.

inputs.description: Maps workflow parameter names (keys) to JSONPath expressions (values) evaluated against the `pull_request` webhook payload. Unresolvable expressions throw at fire time. Max 20 entries.
